title: Satellite-Based Bitcoin Transaction Broadcaster
problem_statement: In regions where internet infrastructure is controlled or destroyed, Bitcoin users cannot broadcast transactions. Blockstream Satellite provides block data downlink but no uplink path for transactions from users without internet access.
proposed_solution: A low-cost device that encodes signed Bitcoin transactions into short burst data messages transmitted via Iridium satellite network. Transactions reach a relay node with internet access for broadcasting to the Bitcoin network.
affected_groups: Humanitarian organizations operating in infrastructure-damaged areas
mvp_scope: Iridium SBD modem connected to a Raspberry Pi running a stripped-down Bitcoin wallet. User signs transaction locally, device transmits via satellite to a relay server that broadcasts to the Bitcoin network.
threat_model: Adversary controlling terrestrial internet infrastructure. Satellite signal jamming (expensive but possible). Relay server operator as potential point of failure.
open_questions: Cost per transaction via Iridium SBD?
Latency acceptable for merchant use cases?
